You can run 19 MPM from 4:30pm to 8:30pm (4 hours) if it is running well.

Chiller no alarms today
The filter on the north side is scratched; we immediately stopped pumping chocolate and are going to replace it with a new one.
Last good check was 8pm
Screen has been replaced.
No metal hits have been reported for any of the TG lines. Team will continue to monitor the screens and metal detectors
The magnet was replaced for inspection and cleaning. No material was observed coming off the Sweco screen.

I asked the team whether we have been following the OPL for using the rubber spatula and they confirmed that we have.
Based on what I observed, my only conclusion is that maybe it’s an infrastructure issue. There are three pipes that discharge chocolate onto the screen: the left pipe is the primary line that deposits chocolate. The primary line delivers the heaviest flow onto the screen, which may be contributing to the issue. The primary is on the left which is where the damage was found on the screen.

The FW is stuck In the fin wheel, the printer is clogged, and  TG 3 has been paused for  one hour
Depositor #4 Centerlines 3rd shift

The film would get caught by the fin wheels because it had excessive build up of glue. OP cleaned wheels and resolved that issue. After that the printer was not printing very well, if anything at all. We took the printing wand out and saw it was clogged, after cleaning the wand carefully the printer was printing correctly. TG3 back up and running.
We’re on depositor 2
Line is back up
ATP spatula correct

no visible jam as indicated

Yes, separate issues. Cold stamp jam and another before vibration 3.

Kimberly Morales if i follow this correctly, it looks like This was two separate things? Depositor 4 hopper level not reading correctly, then a jam at cold stamp? Is Depositor 4 still reading level incorrectly, or just emptying when in auto?

Temper is 0.8 we stopped depositing
Depositing again
The Feastable milk chocolate bar 35g is within weight and measurement range, with no markings or irregularities. The flow wrap is within spec. The temper checks have been in range.

We’re still in the yellow. I did notice that when I opened the doors to tap on the areas I felt a lot of heat. I’ve been leaving the doors open and precooling and outlet temperature has dropped. Is it possible it’s getting too hot in there?
Anything is possible at this point.  Haha, can you send the HMI?
Here’s the trend of the graph since keeping them open
Precooling was at 92.1
Wow. We saw 92+ earlier today, but everything else was way more normal.
Yeah it’s weird we had 91.6-8 and it was fine
Fan has been placed next to it for 15mins and we’ve seen a drop
Good troubleshooting
Thanks I haven’t made adjustments to the HMI because I don’t want to throw too much on it until it cools for a bit longer
Anymore adjustments I should say
We have problem with C6 Belt it try to climb up into the rail, maintenance is working on it.
We got .5 slope for the last two so I lowered mixing water by .2

Can you show the picture of the trend Nathan Gevenois?  

Thank you for all the detail.
Idk all the settings are pretty low right now.
The outlet temperature is good. I just lowered the crystallization
And it took a few minutes for it to flow out
I removed the fan because the exit had hardened chocolate in it
It was worth a try, but doesn't seem to have had the effect we hoped
Nope not at all 😞🤔
I mean the outlet temperature looked great but the temper was still high
Latest temper was .4
We had cold water at 57 for a while. Did we forget to change that after todays recipe oops...
Yeah cold water was at 57 for a couple days before the recipe was loaded this morning.

The absolute lowest mixing water can go to is 81.0 Nathan
What the thought process here?
 
By doing this we are creating more crystals and its no wonder we're getting high temper

Yes but crystallization does something different than mixing water.
Mixing water must stay above phase 4 crystal temps 
Crystallization is meant to stay below phase 4 crystal temp

Yes that is interesting. I apologize for not paying closer attention team.
 
I ask going forward for everyone to continue to reach out to me if they have questions going outside the range given on centerline sheets
Alright so go up to 81? or at least .4 above crystallization?
I was going to ask if that's the case why wasn't anything said before? I will drop the cold water thanks preston
It went from 81.1 at 6:52 to 79.7 at 7:19...

I think the main issue we're dealing with is the pre cooling temp being inconsistent.
I thought we had all come to the same conclusion. 
Pre cooling should bring the chocolate temp NEAR phase 5 crystal temp. 92.9. Thats why we have it at 91.0 on centerline. This one is tricky because of the term "near". 
With it being so inconsistent I like that it was decided to bring it to where we have it. However, when it spikes and goes beyond 92, we destroy phase 5 crystals and then create phase 4 and 3 crystals in every level afterwards which is why we have high temper.
 
So,
To your question Nathan. Watch pre cooling closely. Lower cold water and raise mixing water simultaneously.
I suggest bring cold water to 57 immediately and mixing water up to 80.5 all at once.
Standby for temper to go out of range but in the long run it will help
Nathan I've never been lower than 81 on mixing.
Not deleting my comment either.

No I am correcting my comment. it went down .4 in 27 mins which I have seen you and multiple people do. As far dropping below 81 I asked you yesterday what's the lowest I can go and your response was 79.2
No that is crystallization. Sorry for the confusion
Nathan that is 1.4
Not .4
The 80s were skipped
You’re correct I apologize
We got an alarm, QA and maintenance are notified
We ran at 79.2 crystalization for a few days on 12g . I think it's ok to go that low and give some back to mixing water if possible.

It may last until Saturday night, but it would be better if we are able to fit it into the little bit of downtime for the changeover, or sooner. If the bearing fails, the chance of damaging the belt and contamination increases. I've found the replacement bearings. It would take 30-60 minutes depending on the condition of the shaft. William Curt Chris
One of the rollers will likely need a bearing replacement before long

One of the rollers for the C1 belt (belt after metal detector) is making a grinding noise. I don't see any evidence of metal shavings or other FM, but this may present an issue for production in the near future if not addressed soon. Dakota Adair Curt Willis Caleb York

RTU was frozen and thawing out due to drain pan being full of condensation water, I removed all the ice build up and adjusted the drain pan to allow condensation to drain correctly. It will be some residual drops from insulation but it will stop shortly.
Dave I will order a drain tarp for your maintenance team to use in cases like this, it will help prevent any cross contamination.
Dripping/leak will stop shortly there is still moisture in the insulation that needs to dry up.
Thanks, William
